# What Counts as Proof?

## Level 1 — Mathematical possibility

Shows a mechanism can work under stated assumptions.

## Level 2 — Empirical causal proof

Randomized or controlled experiments show the mechanism changes behaviour.

## Level 3 — Observational statistical proof

Network or time-series evidence shows the observed pattern is unlikely under a null model.

## Level 4 — Case proof

Regulators, platforms, courts, or credible reporting document actual examples.

## Level 5 — Intent proof

Requires direct evidence: contracts, leaked instructions, payment records, admissions, official findings, or internal documents.

## Best paper phrasing

Mathematics proves the mechanism. Experiments prove behavioural susceptibility. Network methods can detect statistically anomalous coordination. Case evidence shows the mechanism occurs in practice. Intent requires direct evidence.
